title: Quiz: Anomaly Detection for Fraud
description: Test your understanding of anomaly detection techniques for fraud detection

questions.question: What is the main advantage of anomaly detection over supervised fraud detection?

options.text: Higher accuracy on known fraud patterns
Works without labeled fraud examples and can detect novel patterns

questions.explanation: Anomaly detection learns 'normal' behavior and flags anything unusual. This allows it to detect novel fraud patterns never seen before, without requiring labeled fraud examples for training.

questions.question: How does Isolation Forest identify anomalies?

options.text: By measuring distance from cluster centers
By counting how many splits are needed to isolate a point

questions.explanation: Isolation Forest works by randomly selecting features and split values. Anomalies are easier to isolate (require fewer splits) because they have unusual feature values, resulting in shorter path lengths.

questions.question: What does an autoencoder use as the anomaly score?

options.text: The size of the encoded representation
The reconstruction error between input and output

questions.explanation: Autoencoders learn to compress and reconstruct normal data. Anomalies have high reconstruction error because the model hasn't learned their patterns, making reconstruction error an effective anomaly score.

questions.question: What is the z-score method for anomaly detection?

options.text: Counting zero values in the data
Measuring how many standard deviations a value is from the mean
Finding the zero point of the distribution

questions.explanation: The z-score measures how many standard deviations a value is from the mean. Values with high absolute z-scores (typically > 3) are considered anomalies as they fall far from the normal distribution center.

questions.question: Anomaly detection methods are immune to concept drift because they don't rely on labeled fraud patterns.
questions.explanation: False. While anomaly detection can adapt better to some changes, it still needs to be updated as 'normal' behavior evolves. Using rolling windows or periodic retraining helps address this, but it's not completely immune.

questions.question: What is the IQR (Interquartile Range) method for detecting outliers?

options.text: Points outside Q1 - 1.5*IQR or Q3 + 1.5*IQR are outliers
Points with more than 1.5 neighbors are outliers
Points in the middle 50% of data are outliers
Points with IQR scores above 1.5 are outliers

questions.explanation: The IQR method defines outliers as points below Q1 - 1.5*IQR (lower fence) or above Q3 + 1.5*IQR (upper fence), where IQR = Q3 - Q1. This is more robust to extreme values than z-score.

questions.question: Why combine multiple anomaly detection methods in an ensemble?

options.text: Different methods capture different types of anomalies

questions.explanation: Different anomaly detection methods have different strengths. Statistical methods catch univariate outliers, Isolation Forest handles high-dimensional data, and autoencoders capture complex patterns. Ensembles provide more robust detection.

questions.question: Which are valid anomaly detection methods? (Select all that apply)
questions.explanation: Isolation Forest, One-Class SVM, and Autoencoders are all valid anomaly detection methods. Random Oversampling is a technique for handling class imbalance, not for detecting anomalies.
